task_completion.description: Navigate to the CAMEO Chemicals website
task_completion.visual_evidence: A web browser is open and displaying the CAMEO Chemicals homepage (cameochemicals.noaa.gov).

task_completion.description: Search for the specified chemicals in the database
task_completion.visual_evidence: The browser shows search results or a specific chemical datasheet (e.g., Chlorine, Ammonia, Phosgene) on the CAMEO Chemicals website.
A text editor (e.g., gedit, mousepad, nano) is open with the filename 'cloud_behavior_assessment.txt' visible in the title bar or status bar.

task_completion.description: Record vapor density, boiling point, and water solubility for the chemicals
task_completion.visual_evidence: The text file contains sections for the chemicals, listing their physical properties (vapor density, boiling point, and water solubility) retrieved from the database.

task_completion.description: Determine and record Cloud Behavior Classification and Rain Scrubbing status
task_completion.visual_evidence: The text file includes the calculated 'Cloud Behavior Classification' (e.g., Ground-Level Hazard, Elevated Disperser) and 'Rain Scrubbing' status (e.g., Rain-scrubbable, Not rain-scrubbable) for the chemicals.

task_completion.description: Save the assessment file to the Desktop
task_completion.visual_evidence: An icon for 'cloud_behavior_assessment.txt' is visible on the Desktop background, or a file manager/save dialog confirms the file is located in the ~/Desktop directory.

integrity.description: The agent used a web browser to access the database
integrity.visual_evidence: A standard web browser interface (like Chromium or Firefox) is visible, showing interaction with the CAMEO Chemicals website rather than using terminal curl/wget commands.

integrity.description: The agent used a text editor to compile the assessment
integrity.visual_evidence: A text editor application is visible on screen for drafting the document, rather than generating the file entirely via background scripts or echo commands.

integrity.description: The recorded data matches the expected database values
integrity.visual_evidence: The values entered in the text file align with the privileged information (e.g., Methyl isocyanate reacting violently with water), indicating genuine data retrieval.

privileged_info_for_vlm:
Expected values based on CAMEO Chemicals:
1. Chlorine: Vapor Density ~2.5, Boiling Point ~-34°C/-30°F, Soluble -> Ground-Level Hazard, Rain-scrubbable.
2. Ammonia: Vapor Density 0.6, Boiling Point ~-33°C/-28°F, Soluble -> Elevated Disperser, Rain-scrubbable.
3. Phosgene: Vapor Density 3.4, Boiling Point ~8°C/47°F, Poorly soluble/hydrolyzes -> Ground-Level Hazard, (Accept either Rain-scrubbable or Not rain-scrubbable).
4. Methyl isocyanate: Vapor Density ~2.0, Boiling Point ~39°C/102°F, Reacts violently with water -> Ground-Level Hazard, Not rain-scrubbable.
5. Sulfur dioxide: Vapor Density ~2.3, Boiling Point ~-10°C/14°F, Soluble -> Ground-Level Hazard, Rain-scrubbable.
